+    "description": "AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity. Requested by 11+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current n8n deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your n8n version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n\nhelm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working n8n installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review n8n configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n\nkubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n\n```\n### Bug Description\n\nHello\n\nI have a problem with AWS EKS Pod Identity."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity",
+        "description": "## Summary\nThis PR is to fix the non-working EKS Pod Identity credentials resolver when using the system credentials option with AWS Credentials.\n\n#21584 introduced support for the `AWS_CONTAINER_AUTHORIZATION_TOKEN_FILE` required for EKS Pod Identity Authentication. There is an existing bug which\n```yaml\n[ ] I have updated to the latest stable version of n8n [please specify version number] and I am still experiencing this issue\n[ ] Please close this issue – I am no longer experiencing it\n[ ] Please close this issue – [please specify a reason]\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n\nkubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "## Summary\nThis PR is to fix the non-working EKS Pod Identity credentials resolver when using the system credentials option with AWS Credentials.\n\n#21584 introduced support for the `AWS_CONTAINER_AUTHORIZATION_TOKEN_FILE` required for EKS Pod Identity Authentication. There is an existing bug which predates #21584 which erroneously adds 'Bearer ' as a prefix to the Authorization header.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "[ ] I have updated to the latest stable version of n8n [please specify version number] and I am still experiencing this issue\n[ ] Please close this issue – I am no longer experiencing it\n[ ] Please close this issue – [please specify a reason]"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
